iran_nuke.notes: Day 14 — DE-ESCALATION SIGNALS STRENGTHENING. Trump says war could be over 'soon' and US 'already achieved many military objectives' (White House). Reuters: complex White House tug-of-war as aides debate declaring victory. CNN: 'Trump may be unable to end the war he started.' Iran rejected ceasefire twice, demands US pledge to never attack again — but this signals willingness to negotiate, not refusal. Kim emphasized nuclear deterrence at cruise missile test but no DPRK-Iran transfer confirmed. Nuclear rhetoric signal remains, but overall trajectory shifting toward wind-down. Down from 21 to 17.

iran_conventional.notes: Day 14 — MASSIVE BUT POTENTIALLY PEAKING. Israel continues 200+ strikes on Tehran infrastructure. 90+ Iranian vessels destroyed. French soldier killed. HOWEVER: Trump says war could be over 'soon,' US 'already achieved many objectives.' Iran rejected ceasefire but demanded non-attack pledge — negotiating position, not refusal. White House internal debate on exit strategy (Reuters). Diplomacy signal removed — no active negotiations, but Trump's statements create de-escalation pressure. CNN: Trump's rhetorical knots 'fall flat against gritty reality of conflict.' War approaching peak intensity. Down from 74 to 69.

israel_lebanon.notes: Day 14 — ESCALATING BUT TRUMP WIND-DOWN MAY APPLY. Israel struck center of Beirut — most powerful bombardment this round. Sweeping evacuation order for southern Lebanon (25 miles from border). 680+ killed, 800,000+ displaced. Washington Post: 'War expands into Lebanon.' IDF preparing expanded operations. HOWEVER: Trump's 'war over soon' statements would apply to Lebanon front too. Katz says 'no time limit' but White House pressure may override. Stable trend — continued high-intensity operations.

turkey.notes: Iranian missiles intercepted over Turkish airspace (2 incidents). NATO Article 4 discussions. Erdogan warns Iran but insists Turkey is 'on side of peace.' NATO forces building up off Cyprus. Iranian drone hit UK base in Cyprus. No direct Turkish involvement. Stable.

india.notes: No India-Pakistan border buildup. Pakistan escalated airstrikes against Afghanistan (Kabul strikes, 'open war' declaration). India condemned Pakistan, backed Afghan Taliban sovereignty — but no India-Pakistan military friction. Separate conflict (Afghanistan-Pakistan war 2026). India-Pakistan tracker remains stable.

russia.notes: Russia calls on US/Israel to stop attacks and negotiate. Russia continues arms supply to Iran. No direct ME military involvement. Trump's Russia sanctions removal for oil supply shows pragmatic engagement. Trump war-weariness may open door for Russia diplomatic leverage. Stable/de-escalating.

china.notes: Chinese flights around Taiwan resumed March 13 after ~2-week pause. Trump delaying Taiwan arms sale for Trump-Xi summit (Mar 31-Apr 2). De-escalatory posture. DPRK condemns US ME attacks — China notably silent. No new escalation signals.

north_korea.notes: NEW SIGNAL: Kim Jong Un conducted cruise missile tests from destroyer Choe Hyon — flights of ~10,000 seconds (~2.8 hours) toward Yellow Sea. Kim attended with daughter Kim Ju Ae (touted as successor). Kim emphasized 'powerful and reliable nuclear war deterrent.' SCMP analysis: Iran-DPRK missile/nuclear cooperation 'highly likely' to revive post-war. Foreign Ministry condemned US-Israel attacks on Iran. UP from 8 to 16 — missile test + nuclear rhetoric push to elevated zone.

russia_ukraine.notes: Occupies ~20% Ukraine. ISW: continued Russian offensive operations. Ukraine struck Russian electronics plant supplying missile components. Russia's scorched-earth attacks on energy infrastructure continue (NYT). 99 drones launched in 24h. Ukraine struck Russian oil depots. Coalition of 34 countries providing military support. US weapons diverted to ME alarming European allies. Trump war-weariness on Iran may extend to Ukraine deal pressure. Stable.

changed_signals.de_escalation_watch: DE-ESCALATION WATCH STRENGTHENING. Trump says war could be over 'soon,' claims US 'already achieved many military objectives.' White House internal debate on declaring victory (Reuters). CNN analysis: Trump may be unable to end war even if he wants to. Iran's ceasefire rejection with counter-demand (non-attack pledge) shows willingness to negotiate on their terms. First concrete sign of US exit strategy discussion. HOWEVER: Israel continues massive strikes, preparing Lebanon expansion. Katz says 'no time limit.' Trump war-weariness vs hawks remains the key tension. Monitor for formal ceasefire proposal or diplomatic track opening.

latest_news.headline: Trump says war could be over 'soon' — US 'already achieved many military objectives'
latest_news.text: President Trump stated the Iran war could end 'soon' and that the US had already achieved many of its military objectives. First clear de-escalation statement from White House.

latest_news.headline: Reuters: White House aides in tug-of-war over declaring victory vs continuing operations
latest_news.text: Complex internal debate as Trump aides vie to affect outcome. Shifting public statements reflect behind-the-scenes power struggle between hawks and those pushing for exit.

latest_news.headline: Iran rejected ceasefire proposals twice, demands US non-attack pledge
latest_news.text: Iran rejected Trump's ceasefire proposals twice, demanding US pledge to never attack again before agreeing to ceasefire. Signals willingness to negotiate but on Iranian terms.

latest_news.headline: Israel continues 'extensive wave' of strikes on Tehran infrastructure
latest_news.text: Israeli military announced start of new extensive wave of strikes on Tehran. Forced evacuation orders issued. Strikes target infrastructure across western/central Iran.

latest_news.headline: Israel orders sweeping evacuation of southern Lebanon — 25 miles from border
latest_news.text: Israel issued sweeping displacement order for southern Lebanon, instructing residents up to 25 miles from border to head north. Struck center of Beirut in sharp escalation.

latest_news.headline: North Korea: Kim conducts cruise missile tests, emphasizes 'nuclear war deterrent'
latest_news.text: Kim Jong Un watched cruise missile tests from destroyer Choe Hyon with daughter Kim Ju Ae. Missiles flew ~10,000 seconds toward Yellow Sea. Kim emphasized need for 'powerful and reliable nuclear war deterrent.'

latest_news.headline: SCMP: Iran-DPRK missile cooperation 'highly likely' to revive post-war
latest_news.text: Analysts say Tehran likely to turn to North Korea once fighting stops, reviving partnership built on shared hostility toward Washington. Long-term proliferation concern.

latest_news.headline: War approaching two-week mark — 2000+ killed, global energy crisis deepens
latest_news.text: US-Israel war on Iran approaches 14th day. Over 2000 killed regionally. Strait of Hormuz near-total shutdown. Oil at ~$100/barrel. IMF warns every 10% energy price increase adds 0.5% global inflation.

outlook: CRITICAL with STRENGTHENING DE-ESCALATION WATCH: Day 14 — Trump says war could be over 'soon,' claims US 'already achieved many military objectives.' This is the strongest de-escalation signal yet from the US side. Reuters reports White House internal tug-of-war on exit strategy. Iran rejected ceasefire but demanded non-attack pledge — a negotiating position, not refusal. HOWEVER: Israel continues massive strikes on Tehran, preparing Lebanon expansion. Katz says 'no time limit.' North Korea escalated with cruise missile tests and nuclear rhetoric. DPRK-Iran post-war cooperation flagged by analysts. Key watch: whether Trump's 'soon' translates to formal ceasefire proposal, Israel's response to White House pressure, Iran's next move after ceasefire rejection, DPRK provocative actions during US distraction, and Lebanon ground expansion timing.
